reasoning: The technical criteria for a recession (two consecutive negative quarters) failed to materialize in early 2025, as Q2 GDP was positive following a modest -0.3% decline in Q1. For the question to resolve YES, either Q3 and Q4 of 2025, or Q4 of 2025 and Q1 of 2026 must both be negative. Since the provided summary does not highlight a negative Q3 report (the advance estimate would have been released in late October 2025), it's highly likely Q3 was positive or sufficiently unalarming. This means a recession would likely need to start specifically in Q4 2025. Given positive full-year GDP forecasts around 2% and the low base rate of a recession starting in any specific quarter (around 3-5%), the probability of the US entering two consecutive negative quarters with a start between late 2024 and end of 2025 is very low.
batch_queries: Will the US enter a recession by end of 2025? (Two quarters negative GDP growth)
